candidates.why: Appears directly in the cost-of-sales section, above gross profit and alongside COGS lines rather than as a child explicitly stated to be included within the selected parent row 'Cost of goods sold'. Under the same-level separate-line rule, this looks like COGS-related D&A that may need to be added separately.
Explicitly tied to R&D, not COGS.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation/Amortization in Salling and admin expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly tied to selling/admin, not COGS.

variables.notes: Selected the standalone operating D&A row in the COGS section because it appears separate from the already selected 'Cost of goods sold' row and there is no more specific COGS-only D&A summary row.

candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as being in R&D, which indicates it is a component of the already selected parent row 'Research and development expenses'. Since XSGA_COMPONENTS already includes that parent row and XRD also separately captures R&D, adding this again would risk double counting.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation/Amortization in Salling and admin expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as being included in selling/admin expenses, indicating it is a component of the already selected parent row 'Selling and administrative expenses', so it should not be added separately.
Generic D&A row appears in the COGS/gross profit section rather than the SG&A section, so it is not the best match for XSGA_DA.

variables.notes: Did not select any XSGA D&A rows because the visible SG&A- and R&D-related D&A lines are explicitly described as being included within the already selected parent expense rows, so separate addition would likely double count.
